authority_profile_short: Curated incident database with strong discovery and comparison value, but not a final authority for legal status or allegation truth.
access_method: Public cite pages, public snapshot surfaces, and bounded route inspection.
reuse_constraints: Do not mirror weekly snapshots or automate bulk pulls until snapshot semantics and rate/terms posture are documented.
language_scope: English-facing interface with global incident coverage.
likely_case_types: Public incident reports, deployment failures, misuse, and controversy traces.
saturation_metric: Fresh bounded live check still returns public cite-page JSON with visible cite-path/template markers; reuse posture remains comparator-only until a stable field-manifest route is documented.
paper_safe_role_now: Comparator baseline and methods support only; not yet elevated to the shared claims layer.
next_verification_move: Verify one snapshot manifest or single-record machine-readable extraction that ties public page labels to field names without broad harvesting.
dashboard_hook: Source-family saturation map; comparator-overlap panel; interoperability readiness board.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for overlap, residual-gap detection, and source-baseline discussion; weak alone for legal/compliance or incident-truth claims.

authority_profile_short: Official institutional monitor with strong policy-facing authority for incident indexing and controlled vocabularies, but not itself a final authority on underlying allegations.
access_method: Public incident pages and unauthenticated JSON facet endpoints under the OECD incidents service.
reuse_constraints: Keep use metadata-first and citation-oriented until list/detail discovery mechanics and rights posture are documented more fully.
language_scope: English-facing interface with international article references and platform vocabularies.
likely_case_types: Public incident summaries, article-linked records, and policy-facing monitoring rows.
saturation_metric: Fresh bounded live check still returns public incident-detail JSON with visible ID/title fields; rights posture remains cautious and metadata-first.
paper_safe_role_now: Comparator baseline and vocabulary bridge support only; not yet elevated to the shared claims layer.
next_verification_move: Trace one public detail page back to a stable list/discovery route and document the minimal safe metadata subset.
dashboard_hook: Country/language coverage comparison; vocabulary bridge; incident-monitor interoperability board.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for comparator metadata, vocabulary bridges, and country/language context; still insufficient alone for strong factual or legal claims.

authority_profile_short: High structured technical authority for public AI vulnerability/report metadata when exact IDs and references are preserved.
access_method: Public website plus openly downloadable GitHub raw JSON and repository contents.
reuse_constraints: MIT-licensed structured metadata lowers reuse friction, but import should stay bounded to IDs, dates, metrics, references, and linked report/vulnerability objects.
likely_case_types: AI vulnerabilities, security edge cases, technical misuse, and failure reports.
saturation_metric: Fresh bounded live check still returns public raw report JSON with stable AVID report ID/date fields; permissive structured metadata posture remains intact.
paper_safe_role_now: Reviewer-safe interoperability/control surface for security and comparator writing, but no current shared claim ID depends on AVID alone.
next_verification_move: Treat the current AVID appendix as the reviewer-safe control surface; only add a third canonical direct join or exact negative-match rule if justified, otherwise return effort to sf08/sf09 authority hardening.
dashboard_hook: Security-edge-case bridge; direct-versus-placeholder join panel; reviewer-safe interoperability appendix.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for technical mechanism, vulnerability metadata, and exact security-row bridges; weak for general incident census or treating placeholder rows as solved joins.

authority_profile_short: High structured authority for adversarial-ML and agentic-security techniques, mitigations, and case-study relations.
access_method: Public website plus Apache-licensed GitHub YAML/data distribution.
reuse_constraints: Apache-2.0 lowers reuse friction for technique/mitigation/case-study metadata; do not overstate case studies as exhaustive incident coverage.
likely_case_types: Adversarial techniques, mitigations, case-study relations, and agentic/security anomaly structure.
saturation_metric: Fresh bounded live check still returns public distributed ATLAS YAML; the deprecation banner remains visible and `version: 5.6.0` still appears, so structured reuse stays valid but version status must travel with citations.
paper_safe_role_now: Technique-context comparator support for security writing, but no current shared claim ID depends on ATLAS alone.
next_verification_move: Treat the current ATLAS appendix as the reviewer-safe control surface; only add a defensible exact case-study ID or stronger independent anchor for cautious rows if justified, otherwise return effort to sf08/sf09 authority hardening.
dashboard_hook: Security/agentic crosswalk; technique-coverage heatmap; case-context-versus-cautious-context panel.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for security-technique structure, mitigation framing, and operational technique-context joins; weak for non-security completeness or direct case-study equivalence claims.

authority_profile_short: Secondary but important structured authority for risk and harm definitions; not an incident-truth source.
reuse_constraints: Use as attributed taxonomy/definition layer only; verified Google Sheets workbook export allows bounded structured tab/slice reuse, but mirror-specific access and row-level event equivalence claims remain out of scope.
likely_case_types: Risk classes, hazard definitions, and residual-category comparison support.
saturation_metric: Fresh bounded live check plus crosswalk verification now confirms the public /risks page exposes CC BY 4.0 dataset metadata and at least one anonymously downloadable Google workbook/CSV export, while the named SharePoint mirror remains sign-in gated.
paper_safe_role_now: Methods/taxonomy support only; now safe for bounded structured workbook/taxonomy overlay description, but still not elevated to the shared claims layer.
next_verification_move: Import one deliberately tiny MIT AI Risk workbook tab or taxonomy slice with explicit provenance, sheet naming, and CC BY note; keep mirror-specific access asymmetry and row-level incident non-equivalence explicit.
dashboard_hook: Taxonomy heatmap; residual-category overlay; methods table on definition-layer comparators; workbook-route readiness note.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for methods, taxonomy, and harm-definition claims; now also supports a bounded structured-workbook overlay description, but remains invalid as event evidence or blanket mirroring permission.

authority_profile_short: Secondary conceptual authority for harm framing and related-work comparison, but not machine-ready incident evidence.
access_method: Public Georgetown CSET article surface plus exact public PDF/README anchors captured in local bridge work.
reuse_constraints: Use citation/manual mapping only; no explicit open machine-reuse grant or row-level public dataset route was verified locally.
likely_case_types: Harm framing, interpretive taxonomy support, and related-work positioning.
saturation_metric: Fresh bounded live check still returns the public CSET overview article shell; the family remains manual-citation-only with no improved permissive reuse signal.
paper_safe_role_now: Appendix-only methods/related-work support; not a shared empirical claim family.
next_verification_move: Keep the family explicitly outside machine-ingest claims and add a second low-drift reviewer pass before any row-level posture aggregate is exposed.
dashboard_hook: Harm-taxonomy note; conceptual-support panel; related-work table; Route-A appendix-only methods note.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for appendix-only harm-posture interpretation and related-work positioning; invalid as event evidence or ingest-ready comparator.

authority_profile_short: High authority for official actions, allegations, orders, fines, guidance, and procedural posture within issuing jurisdiction.
access_method: Official regulator portals, enforcement pages, press releases, decisions, and linked official documents.
reuse_constraints: Public-document use only; preserve original-language nuance and narrow posture-based wording.
likely_case_types: Enforcement actions, deceptive-marketing cases, privacy breaches, official warnings, and procedural escalations.
saturation_metric: 11-row shared authoritative core now counts 8 direct-document-grade rows across Italy, the United States, the United Kingdom, and promoted FTC non-finance official rows, while the court-family blocker remains tracked separately under sf08.
paper_safe_role_now: Core authoritative-event family for the shared authoritative package plus multiple shard-specific official-response and detector-governance claims.
next_verification_move: Add one non-U.S./non-UK follow-through row tied to a public record or court-facing document to reduce current geography concentration, while keeping the court-family bottleneck on exact sf08 depth separate.
dashboard_hook: Authority-weighted evidence matrix; regulator-action timeline; jurisdiction map.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Best current family for upgrading weak media or company claims into paper-grade bounded statements.

authority_profile_short: Highest authority for filings, judgments, consent decrees, and procedural posture when records are official and public.
access_method: Public court databases, judgment portals, tribunal repositories, or official DOJ/agency-linked docket materials.
reuse_constraints: Respect access limits; no scraping behind logins, paywalls, sealed, or private attachments.
likely_case_types: Litigation, injunctions, complaints, decrees, judgments, and review of automated/public AI decisions.
saturation_metric: 1 DOJ-linked indictment PDF remains image-only on this host with stable partial caption OCR (`25 Cr.` only) and 1 EEOC filing/settlement pair still exposes Civil Action No. 1:22-cv-02565 only at agency-summary level; bounded EEOC site search, named public mirrors, CourtListener, and later Bing browser-UI discovery probes still yielded no open court-originating complaint, decree, docket entry, or judicial order text.
paper_safe_role_now: Top-priority hostile-reviewer blocker because stronger court text would immediately harden the authoritative shared claims layer.
next_verification_move: Either capture one open complaint, consent decree, docket entry, or judicial order text for the iTutorGroup row from a genuinely different public court-facing surface or clearly labeled open mirror, or use a materially different OCR stack / another public exact-caption source before retrying the Nate suffix; reuse the existing negative-result ledgers so failed EEOC newsroom/node/search, named public-mirror, CourtListener, and Bing browser-UI challenge paths are not repeated.
dashboard_hook: Regulator-to-court escalation matrix; procedural-posture timeline; Route-B blocker panel.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Critical for hostile-reviewer resilience where regulator or agency summaries alone are too thin.

authority_profile_short: Medium-to-high authority for what a public body disclosed or procured; weak for deployment impact, legality, or efficacy unless corroborated.
access_method: Official registers, procurement portals, tender notices, award pages, contract pages, and public-body project pages.
reuse_constraints: Metadata-first extraction; do not infer deployment, legality, or AI capability beyond source text.
jurisdiction_scope: Country, regional, and municipal public-sector jurisdictions.
update_cadence: Periodic / batch releases varying by portal.
likely_case_types: Algorithm registers, procurement anomalies, AI system disclosures, and public deployment surfaces.
saturation_metric: 2 municipal public AI register rows still anchor the family, but the bounded non-register side now also includes 1 Slovenian police-plus-CORDIS official companion pair, 2 UK regulator-document packages, 1 Danish Datatilsynet governance row plus 2 exact-row Retsinformation legal-basis companions whose readable official legal text is now locally verified on this host, 1 official UFSN taskforce-report companion, 1 Helsinki same-buyer procurement companion, and Amsterdam's explicit same-buyer Azure/Public Cloud family bridge; a fresh bounded same-lineage official-route probe across Datatilsynet outbound links, su.dk / ufm.dk / ufsn.dk search shells, and udbud.dk's JavaScript-gated procurement shell yielded no later Danish operational/procurement/implementation/outcome document. Buyer-side/public-impact proof is still missing.
paper_safe_role_now: Core public-sector disclosure family for the shared public-sector package, now spanning register, regulator-document, official project-plus-EU companion, exact-row legal-basis-chain, exact-row readable legal-text verification, and exact-row official planning-document subtypes.
next_verification_move: Use the new no-yield audit to avoid replaying the same Datatilsynet outbound links and official site-search shells; count progress only if one materially different Amsterdam/Helsinki buyer-side or public-impact companion lands, or one later Danish operational/procurement/implementation/evaluation document appears from a genuinely different public route.
dashboard_hook: Jurisdiction/language map; disclosure-field completeness chart; register-versus-project-page-versus-regulator panel; Route-B blocker panel; public-sector proof-ceiling comparator; governance/planning follow-through note; rare-language legal-text readability control.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Useful for public-sector disclosure heterogeneity, bounded official-companion depth, exact rare-language legal-text quoting for the Danish governance sidecar, and explicit route-exhaustion evidence; still not enough for procurement/deployment proof, legality, impact, or efficacy claims.

authority_profile_short: Primary only for what the issuer disclosed; self-interested and incomplete for broader truth claims.
access_method: Public status pages, transparency reports, release notes, product incident notes, GitHub advisories/issues.
reuse_constraints: Public notices only; no private support content or hidden dashboards.
language_scope: Mostly English with some localized notices.
likely_case_types: Outages, rollbacks, remediation notices, policy changes, and deployment pauses.

paper_safe_role_now: Supports the security shared claim through remediation and disclosure-timing context, but remains below independent-event authority.
next_verification_move: Normalize ToolHive into the issuer-notice ledger if needed, otherwise reopen sf10 only if another notice-depth threshold changes state while preserving advisory-vs-release-note and issuer-vs-independent-source distinctions.
dashboard_hook: Remediation timeline overlay; issuer-vs-independent-source comparison table; security source-family front door panel.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Useful for timing, remediation visibility, and issuer-versus-independent-source comparisons; stronger than before because Langflow now has formal advisory depth and the CrewAI pair now has exact-fix visibility, but the family still should not stand alone for incident characterization or prevalence.

reuse_constraints: Preserve bounded metadata and defensive lessons only; exclude exploit payloads and dangerous reproduction detail.
language_scope: Mostly English with some multilingual advisories.
likely_case_types: Prompt injection, exfiltration, model inversion, unsafe agent tooling, red-team findings, and coordinated disclosures.
saturation_metric: 11-row live security package still sits at 3 core / 8 appendix / 1 gap; the best exact scholarly attach point now combines exact-CVE preprint fit with published AIES disclosure-context support, and a later lane-front-door currentness audit synchronized lane-local route-entry surfaces to that closure logic, but no live row yet has venue-grade incident-specific technical-case closure and the trust-boundary slice remains a bounded three-row surface (ToolHive, Playwright MCP/deployment, and one seeded non-MCP PraisonAI row).
paper_safe_role_now: Core security family for the shared agentic-security package and a Route-B blocker with one remaining family-level peer-reviewed gap plus a bounded three-row trust-boundary slice; the newer published disclosure-context signal and later lane-front-door currentness repair sharpen route order but do not close the row-level technical-case rule.
next_verification_move: Keep the synchronized lane-front-door audit as the current sf11 entry surface, but count progress only if one live row gains incident-specific peer-reviewed, workshop, or conference-grade technical case support; after that, pursue broader trust-boundary authority beyond the current bounded three-row slice or a stronger independent attachment for the memory/state-contamination route.
dashboard_hook: Security-source authority matrix; advisory-to-CVE linkage graph; remediation-visibility overlay; Route-B security hardening panel.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Useful for mechanism discovery, source-independence comparison, coordination posture, remediation-visibility comparisons, and reviewer-safe route explanation; stronger again because lane-local route-entry surfaces now match the live closure logic, but the family still cannot be narrated as having venue-grade incident-specific technical-case closure or broad prevalence support.

authority_profile_short: Secondary corroborative authority valuable for discovery, local context, and timeline stitching, but weaker than official sources.
access_method: Public news sites, investigative outlets, archives, and broadcaster pages.
reuse_constraints: Use quotations sparingly; preserve outlet/date/URL and do not treat visibility as prevalence.
language_scope: Multilingual, including underrepresented and local-language reporting.
likely_case_types: Discovery leads, local context, public reactions, timeline stitching, and cases absent from English summaries.
saturation_metric: 6 local leads across Finland, Lithuania, Slovenia, and Estonia; 1 lead already upgraded from media/discovery friction to a direct official source.
paper_safe_role_now: Core multilingual discovery-and-coverage family for the shared multilingual package, with explicit translation-review caution.
next_verification_move: Resolve one additional direct source from Finland, Lithuania, or Estonia and record whether the stronger authority comes from broadcaster, police, court, or agency sources.
dashboard_hook: Jurisdiction/language map; lead-to-authority upgrade funnel; rare-language methods note.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for discovery, coverage-gap analysis, and rare-language methods; still weak alone for strong legal or normative claims.

authority_profile_short: Medium-to-high authority for retractions, correction notices, benchmark-integrity notes, and scholarly dispute records depending on source.
access_method: Public journal notices, publisher pages, DOI registries, retraction databases, and institutional reports.
reuse_constraints: Public/citation-ready sources preferred; preserve publisher-specific reuse terms.
language_scope: Mostly English with some multilingual notices.
likely_case_types: Retracted AI papers, benchmark contamination, fabricated evaluation claims, and research-integrity anomalies.
saturation_metric: 7 locally classified rows now split into 5 evidence-depth classes: three direct publisher-originating notice-text exemplars across archive-resolved NEJM plus two Springer surfaces, one archived residual platform-action exemplar tied to an exact SSRN/arXiv pair, one mediated SAGE row, and two still-weak specialist/discovery rows.
paper_safe_role_now: Core research-integrity/procedural-contamination family for the shared source-depth package, now with a residual action-record sidecar that separates one escaped fake-article-network exemplar from the still-weak floor.
next_verification_move: Capture one direct SAGE journal/publisher notice or another non-archived cross-publisher scholarly action record, while preserving the one-archived-action-versus-two-still-weak residual split.
dashboard_hook: Research-integrity dashboard facet; publisher-notice upgrade funnel; scholarly-integrity timeline; archive-versus-live route panel; residual action-vs-recurrence badge.
reviewer_safe_interpretation: Strong for bounded claims about article identification, retraction/correction surfaces, publisher-notice depth, one archived residual platform-action exemplar, and source-resolution friction; weaker for editorial reasoning where direct notice text is absent or archive-only.

authority_profile_short: Low authority alone; discovery surface only until independently corroborated.

reuse_constraints: Avoid humiliation, doxxing, or sensationalism; preserve only public-safe metadata.
